title: Beat the ATS
content:
Most mid-to-large employers run your CV through an **Applicant Tracking System** before any human sees it. The ATS scans for keywords from the job description and ranks you on the overlap. Miss the keywords and a qualified application can be filtered out.

How it actually works: matching is mostly **literal**. If the posting says "stakeholder management" and you wrote "client relations", the ATS may not connect them. So:

1. **Read the posting and pull the keywords** - the hard skills, tools, certifications, and exact phrases in the requirements.
2. **Mirror them where they're genuinely true of you** - in your skills section and woven into your experience bullets. Use both the spelled-out term and the acronym ("Search Engine Optimization (SEO)").
3. **Don't stuff.** Repeating a keyword 10 times or hiding white text gets flagged and reads badly to humans. Use terms naturally.
4. **Tailor per application.** Different postings for the same role use different wording. A generic CV underperforms a tailored one.

Use this app's **ATS Checker** to paste a job description and see your match score and the exact keywords you're missing - then add only the ones you honestly have.

---
